mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) signal strengthening consumer demand and corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism operates through improved profit expectations and reduced recession risk.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 339299M as of March 2005 reflects sustained consumer spending
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS momentum and 20-day forward returns falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive RSAFS growth fails to produce positive forward returns in > 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 2.5%, the Fed is in a tightening or restrictive stance that increases discount rates and borrowing costs, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as equity valuations compress.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 2.63% as of March 2005 is above historical lows
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS > 2.5%, S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward returns is positive and significant (p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment (UNRATE) indicates labor market tightening and rising wage pressures that compress profit margins and increase inflation expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices earnings and discount rates.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls below 5.0% for 2 consecutive months
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with wage-price spiral risk
failure_condition: When UNRATE < 5.0% and CPIAUCSL is rising, S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of instances, or the joint interaction coefficient between UNRATE and CPIAUCSL is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) signals strengthening economic capacity utilization and corporate output growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as the market reprices near-term earnings expectations upward.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 118.527 as of March 2005 reflects sustained industrial activity
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO momentum and 5-day forward returns falls below 0.01 over any 1-year rolling window, or positive INDPRO growth fails to produce positive forward returns in > 52% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly signal broad-based demand strength across residential and consumer sectors. When both rise together, the multiplier effect on corporate earnings and GDP growth produces positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST > 2100K units and RSAFS > 335000M in same month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 2195K and RSAFS at 339299M as of February-March 2005
failure_condition: When both HOUST > 2100K and RSAFS > 335000M, S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of instances, or the interaction coefficient is not significantly positive (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) accelerates above 200K per month, signaling robust labor demand and consumer income growth that supports equity valuations through higher expected corporate revenues and reduced recession risk over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly change > 200K for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: When PAYEMS growth > 200K month-over-month, S&P 500 produces negative 60-day returns in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC between PAYEMS growth and forward returns is not significantly positive (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL rising above 2.5% year-over-year forces the Fed to maintain or raise FEDFUNDS, creating a regime where real discount rates remain elevated and equity valuations face downward pressure, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 192.0 and FEDFUNDS at 2.63% as of February-March 2005
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL inflation > 2.5% and FEDFUNDS > 2.0%, S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of instances, or the interaction coefficient between inflation and rates is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month while unemployment (UNRATE) rises simultaneously, signaling stagflationary conditions or demand destruction that reduces corporate earnings and increases equity risk premiums, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls > 0.5% month-over-month AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 118.527 and UNRATE at 5.2% as of March 2005
failure_condition: When INDPRO declines > 0.5% and UNRATE rises > 0.2pp simultaneously, S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances, or the interaction coefficient is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).
